revision.text:
{{otheruses|the current version of the city|the original version of the city|Memory of Old Lion's Arch|the city during [[Scarlet Briar|Scarlet's]] attack|The Battle For Lion's Arch}} {{Location infobox | objectives = y | type = City | within = Kryta | location tree = Tyria; Kryta | connections = [[Lornar's Pass]] (E)
[[Gendarran Fields]] (N)
[[Bloodtide Coast]] (S)
[[Lion's Arch Aerodrome]] (S)

[[Chantry of Secrets]]
[[Crystal Oasis]]
[[Divinity's Reach]]
[[Hoelbrak]]
[[The Grove]]
[[Rata Sum]]
[[Southsun Cove]]
[[Arborstone]]
[[The Wizard's Tower]]
[[Shipwreck Strand]] | pets = [[Juvenile Armor Fish|Armor Fish]]
[[Juvenile Blue Jellyfish|Blue Jellyfish]]
[[Juvenile Krytan Drakehound|Krytan Drakehound]]
[[Juvenile Lynx|Lynx]]
[[Juvenile Pig|Pig]]
[[Juvenile Shark|Shark]] | id = 50 | region id = 4 | continent id = 1 }} {{quotation|In-game description|Lion's Arch, built upon the wreckage of the Krytan City of the same name, has grown and prospered in the years since the rise of the Orrian Dragon. Founded by pirates and dependent on trade, the city has become a common ground for all the races of Tyria.}} [[Lion's Arch]] (sometimes shortened to '''LA''') is a coastal [[city|city zone]] in the [[Kryta]] region. It is the most diverse and cosmopolitan city in [[Tyria]], a melting pot where all [[race]]s gather and trade. It owes no allegiance to any race or nation, but stands on its own – and does so by virtue of its active navy, its financial strength, the intelligence and cunning of its leaders. Lion's Arch figures prominently in the game's storyline. One of the main themes of ''[[Guild Wars 2]]'' is bringing together disparate, even opposed groups in order to fight a greater enemy: the [[Elder Dragon]]s. Alone, no race will survive their awakening. Only together can the races of Tyria survive. Nowhere in Tyria is that belief more strongly held than the city of Lion's Arch. As the cultural and economic hub, Lion's Arch serves as the central focus of holidays and festivities such as [[Halloween]]. During Halloween, [[Mad King Thorn]] walks the streets of Lion's Arch telling jokes, and the city is full of spooky, dark, and pumpkin decor. == Getting there == Note that [https://help.guildwars2.com/hc/articles/230165307 Free to play accounts] can only access Lion's Arch at level 35. * From [[Gendarran Fields]] — [[portal (zone)|portal]] in the southeastern part of the map, south of the {{waypoint|Cornucopian Fields Waypoint}}. * From [[Lornar's Pass]] — portal in the central western part of the map, south of the {{waypoint|Durmand Priory Waypoint}}. * From [[Bloodtide Coast]] — portal in the northern part of the map, north of the {{waypoint|Archen Foreland Waypoint}}. * From [[City#Capital cities|capital cities]]: ** [[Black Citadel]] — [[asura gate]] on the ledge northwest of the [[Imperator's Core]], near the {{waypoint|Ligacus Aquilo Waypoint}}. ** [[Divinity's Reach]] — asura gate in the southeastern part of the [[District Promenade]], the central area of the city, near the {{waypoint|Palace Waypoint}}. ** [[The Grove]] — asura gate in the [[Marshaling Field]] on the top layer of the city, the closest waypoint is {{waypoint|Upper Commons Waypoint}}. ** [[Hoelbrak]] — asura gate in the [[Peeta's Gate]], western part of the city, near {{waypoint|Peeta's Waypoint}}. ** [[Rata Sum]] — asura gate on the top level in the northern part of the city, near {{waypoint|Magustan Court Waypoint}}. * From [[Lion's Arch Aerodrome]] — portal in the northern part of the map. * From [[Memory of Old Lion's Arch]] — using the [[Lion's Pride]] [[Gizmo]] in old Lion's Arch to travel to the modern version of Lion's Arch. * [[Southsun Cove]] — asura gate in the southeastern part of the map, near {{waypoint|Pearl Islet Waypoint}}. * [[Arborstone]] — asura gate in the southeastern part of the map, on the second level. * [[The Wizard's Tower]] — portal in the eastern part of the [[Outer Ring]], at {{point of interest|Lion's Arch Connector}}. * [[Shipwreck Strand]] — portal in the northeastern part of the map, near {{waypoint|Dark Leviathan Waypoint}}. * Using portals or portal managers in most of the [[lounge]]s, accessible by purchasing a pass in the [[Gem Store]]. == History == === Early years === {{section-stub|location|Add lore from Sea of Sorrows and Guild Wars 1.}} Lion's Arch went through multiple changes over the years since its days as the capital of [[Kryta]] in the original ''[[gw1:Guild Wars|Guild Wars]]'' to its current incarnation as a free city in ''[[Guild Wars 2]]''. === City of Pirates === {{section-stub|location|Add lore from personal story like Claw Island etc.}} [[File:Escape from Lion's Arch 1.jpg|thumb|250px|Many of the buildings in the city were made from shipwrecks.]] In 1219 [[Mouvelian calendar|AE]], the [[Elder Dragon]] [[Zhaitan]] woke from its slumber. The [[Rising of Orr]] created a great flood which destroyed and completely submerged the old capital. The ruins of Lion's Arch were utilized as a safe harbor by ships looking to escape the horrors of the sea. A decade later, in 1230 AE, the wreckage of [[old Lion's Arch]] was slowly turned into a refuge for [[Corsair]]s and other [[pirate]]s, smugglers, and other independent vessels. Shanty towns were created, and as the waters slowly receded, a settlement formed that owed no allegiance to Kryta. This settlement was unified by a [[human]] named [[Cobiah Marriner]], who fought against the scourge of [[Ruins of Orr|Orr]] alongside [[charr]], [[asura]], [[norn]], and any other race that would lend their vessels to the cause. Out of that staunch and allied front, the city of Lion's Arch was reborn. For generations, the people of Lion's Arch were on the front line of the war with Zhaitan, the dragon of Orr. Their city was destroyed, only to be rebuilt in defiance of the Elder Dragon's power. The elite [[Lionguard]] built a fortress on [[Claw Island]], a small body of land in the harbor, in order to defend against Zhaitan's attacks. No ship sails from their docks without a wary eye south toward the dark waters of the undead. They live each day with the knowledge that they are a bastion against the destruction that the Elder Dragon would bring—regardless of whether or not the other nations of the world recognize, or care about, their ongoing struggle. For decades, Lion's Arch has been run by the [[Captain's Council]], a committee of wealthy merchants and sea captains. Those who wish to take an active hand in the city's governance must prove themselves regardless of their race and heritage. They must be respected as a noted ship commander, and they must have contributed to the city's growth or health. If they are seen as a leader in the city, then they may purchase one of the seats on the Captain's Council—when one becomes available. This system of political governance leads to a great deal of one-upmanship among the local captains and on the council itself. === Battle for Lion's Arch === {{section-stub|location|Add Season 1 lore about Mad King Thorn's invasion, Dragon Bash, murder of Theo Ashford etc.}} [[File:Battle for Lion's Arch screenshot 9.jpg|thumb|250px|Lion's Arch during Scarlet's attack.]] In 1327 AE, after months of preparation, the arch-villain [[Scarlet Briar]] descended on Lion's Arch with all of her [[Scarlet's Alliance|deadly forces]]. The city was razed and the smoldering husk of the proud port city was occupied, and it was weeks before the siege was broken. After days of battle, Scarlet was slain in the skies above the city aboard her airship, ''[[the Breachmaker]]''. Word of Scarlet's demise has spread like wildfire throughout the wards of the city. With renewed vigor, the heroes of Lion's Arch, the Lionguard, and [[Order]] forces have driven out the remainder of Scarlet's armies, their morale and ranks shattered. Cheers and celebrations erupted at the refugee camps and nigh across all of Tyria. All the while, ''the Breachmaker''—the enormous airship looming over [[Sanctum Harbor]]—continued to drill uncontrollably before it mysteriously exploded a few days after hitting a magical leyline which was Scarlet's target. The resulting blast caused the pieces of the Breachmaker to be flung out across the city—mostly concentrated around the harbor but with debris landing as far out as the [[Farshore Ward]] and scarring the landscape. With the pieces being so immensely large, the resultant rebuilding of Lion's Arch was taking place around these seemingly immovable chunks of metal. === New Lion's Arch === {{section-stub|location|Add possible later developments in the city etc.}} Lion's Arch [[Game updates/2015-06-23|finished its rebuild]] in 1328 AE. Parts of ''[[The Breachmaker]]'' were molded into new metal used to build several new structures, such as an aerodrome, a fortress, turrets, a new harbor with a shipyard amongst others. Some of the new buildings and structures are based on sea creatures and the city now has more of a maritime flair than the previous pirate flair. Several points of interest also had their names modified or replaced. The previously destroyed waypoint in Fort Marriner was restored and is now usable. Many citizens and services were moved including those for [[guild]]s that are now at [[Guild Initiative Headquarters]]. In 1330 AE, many [[Elona|elonian]] refugees flee to Lion's Arch due to [[Balthazar]] wages war against the Elder Dragons in the [[Crystal Desert]]. == Unique features == Lion's Arch is unique among the cities of Tyria since it is the central trade and cultural hub. It has features not found in other cities: * {{map icon|asura gate}} [[Asura gate]]s ** To the capital [[City|cities]] of all [[playable race]]s, located in [[Coriolis Plaza]]. ** To far-flung destinations including [[Pearl Islet]] in [[Southsun Cove]], [[Arborstone]] in [[Cantha]], [[The Wizard's Tower]] in the [[Horn of Maguuma]] and [[Shipwreck Strand]] in [[Castora]], located in Coriolis Plaza. ** To [[World versus World]], located in [[Fort Marriner]]. ** To [[Structured PvP]], located in Fort Marriner. * Portals exiting within or near each of the 3 [[Order]]s. Reach ** [[Chantry of Secrets]] via the portal within the [[safe house]] at [[Order of Whispers Headquarters]]. ** [[Durmand Priory]] via the portal north-east of Cavern [[Waypoint]]. ** [[Vigil Keep]] via the portal to [[Cornucopian Fields]]. * {{map icon|dungeon}} [[Dungeon Transportation|Connection]] to the [[Dungeons]], located in Fort Marriner. * {{map icon|raid}} Connection to the [[Lion's Arch Aerodrome]] and the raid portals located within. * {{map icon|fractals of the mists}} [[Fractals of the Mists]], located in Fort Marriner. * {{map icon|strike mission}} [[Old Lion's Court]], located in [[Grand Piazza]]. * {{vendor|dungeon merchant}} [[Dungeon Armor and Weapons]], located in Fort Marriner. Sells items from all dungeons. * {{vendor|guild commendation}} [[Guild Commendation Trader]], located at Guild Initiative Headquarters. * {{vendor|ectoplasm vendor}} [[Apprentice Mekteki|Ectoplasm vendor]], located in [[White Crane Terrace]] * {{vendor|collections instructor}} [[Grandmaster Craftsman Hobbs|Legendary Weapons crafting vendor]] (the only one in the entire game) * {{vendor|collections instructor}} [[Scholar Mossi|Legendary Weapons trophy collector]] (the only one in the entire game) * {{icon|mystic forge}} [[Mystic Forge]], located in [[Trader's Forum]] * [[File:Feat chest (map icon).png|20px]] [[Tri-key Chest]], located in [[the Undermarket]] == Locations == {| {{STDT|sortable pve locations}} {{Location table header|nolevel=1}} |- | [[Bloodcoast Breakfast]]", "icon": "Personal waypoint (map icon).png" }, { "coord": [48792.9, 32006.8], "name": "Landmark", "text": "[[Phoenix Dawn]]", "icon": "Personal waypoint (map icon).png" }, { "coord": [49051.9, 31800.8], "name": "Landmark", "text": "[[Claw Island Portage]]", "icon": "Personal waypoint (map icon).png" }, { "coord": [48573.9, 31276.8], "name": "Landmark", "text": "[[Trader Jikk's]]", "icon": "Personal waypoint (map icon).png" }, { "coord": [48342.9, 31735.8], "name": "Landmark", "text": "[[Diverse Ledges]]", "icon": "Personal waypoint (map icon).png" }, { "coord": [48341.9, 31363.8], "name": "Landmark", "text": "[[Maeva's house]]", "icon": "Personal waypoint (map icon).png" }, { "coord": [48608.8, 30851.5], "name": "Faction Provisioner", "text": "[[Skritt Trader (Lion's Arch)|Skritt Trader]]", "icon": "Faction Provisioner (map icon).png" }, { "coord": [48633.7, 30866.2], "name": "Collections Instructor", "text": "[[Legendary Collector Youngblood]]", "icon": "Basic Collections.png" }, { "coord": [48137.9, 30868.8], "name": "Festival Merchants", "text": "[[Festival Merchant]]", "icon": "Festival Vendor (map icon).png" }, { "coord": [48162.9, 30865.8], "name": "Lost and Found Shinies", "text": "[[Sarettokk]]", "icon": "HoMPS merchant (map icon).png" }, { "coord": [48163.9, 30826.8], "name": "Birthday Vendor", "text": "[[Birthday Vendor]]", "icon": "Birthday Vendor.png" }, { "coord": [48164.9, 30811.8], "name": "Tri-key Chest", "text": "[[Tri-key Chest]]", "icon": "Feat chest (map icon).png" }, }} == Pets == ;[[Juvenile Armor Fish]] * [[Sanctum Harbor]] (near the [[Wreckage of the Breachmaker]]) ;[[Juvenile Blue Jellyfish]] * [[Sanctum Harbor]] (southeast of [[Old Lion's Arch]]) ;[[Juvenile Krytan Drakehound]] * [[Captain Theo Ashford Memorial Bridge]] (named [[Tomme's Dog]] in the bridge's archway directly west of the PoI, appears shortly after someone has acquired [[Tomme's Cat]]) ;[[Juvenile Lynx]] * [[Captain Theo Ashford Memorial Bridge]] (named [[Tomme's Cat]] in the bridge's archway directly west of the PoI, appears shortly after someone has acquired [[Tomme's Dog]]) ;[[Juvenile Pig]] * [[Farshore Ward]] (on the farm above Cavern Waypoint) ;[[Juvenile Shark]] * [[Sharkmaw Caverns]] (in the cave's lake) == Diving location == * At the very top of the lighthouse (west of {{map icon|poi}} [[Phoenix Roost]]) right next to the Vista == [[Jumping puzzles]] == * [[Troll's Revenge]] * [[Urmaug's Secret]] * [[Weyandt's Revenge]] ;Historical * [[Troll's End]] (available in the [[Memory of Old Lion's Arch]]) == Gallery == File:Western Ward (aftermath).jpg|The wrecked Western Ward. File:Lion's Gate destroyed.jpg|The wreckage of Lion's Gate. File:Retribution.jpg|[[Retribution (story)|Retribution]] loading screen. File:"Lion's Arch Auction House" concept art.jpg|Lion's Arch auction house by Levi Hopkins. File:"Lion's Arch Anchor Lighthouse" concept art.jpg|Lion's Arch Anchor Lighthouse by Levi Hopkins. File:Suspicious Activity.jpg|[[Suspicious Activity]] loading screen. File:Lions Arch rebuild(3) concept art.jpg|Rebuilt Lion's Arch. ;Historical loading screens File:Lion's Arch loading screen (original).jpg|Original loading screen. File:Lion's Arch (Season 2).jpg|Throughout [[Living World Season 2]]. File:Lion's Arch (Dragon Bash) loading screen.jpg|[[Dragon Bash 2013]]. File:Lion's Arch (Wintersday) loading screen.jpg|[[Wintersday|Wintersday 2012, 2013]]. File:Lion's Arch (Halloween) loading screen.jpg|[[Halloween|Halloween 2012, 2013]]. ;Holiday loading screens File:Lion's Arch Halloween 2015 loading screen.png|[[Halloween]] loading screen. ;Previous maps File:Lion's Arch map (original).jpg|Map of Lion's Arch during [[Edge of the Mists (living world)]]. File:Lion's Arch map back trading.jpg|Map of Lion's Arch during [[Living World Season 2]]. File:Lion's Arch map aftermath.jpg|Version of the above map (likely unused ingame) but with underwater structures visible. File:Lion's Arch map remove parts.jpg|Map of Lion's Arch during [[Game updates/2015-05-19|reconstruction]]. ;Iconography Lion's Arch emblem.png|The emblem of Lion's Arch. == Related achievements == * {{achievement|Friends in High Places|category=Community}} * {{achievement|Lion's Arch Exterminator|category=Explorer}} == Notes == * There was an extensive [[The Bilge|sewage system]] beneath the city previous to its rebuild in 1328 AE. * As this isn't a racial [[city]], there is no [[home instance]]. * During the original Wintersday, the city was visited by [[Toymaker Tixx]], and [[Snowman (NPC)|Snowmen]] roamed the snow-covered, festively decorated city to give presents. All subsequent releases of Wintersday take place in [[Divinity's Reach]]. * The map used to be able to be broken out of by going to the [[Shuttered Gate]] and relogging in the right corner of it. An invisible wall was placed along it in a bug fix introduced in autumn 2017. * During [[Living World Season 1]], through the original [[Escape from Lion's Arch]] and the [[Battle for Lion's Arch/archive|Battle for Lion's Arch]] episodes, the usual city services such as [[crafting station]]s and [[trading post]] access were moved to the [[Vigil Headquarters|Vigil]] and [[Durmand Priory (location)|Durmand Priory]] headquarters. * Map complete rewards for Lion's Arch is experience and a transmutation charge. *The pirate's version/second age of Lion's Arch, that was removed from the game during the 2014 [[Escape from Lion's Arch]] release, was restored in the form of the [[Memory of Old Lion's Arch]] lounge during the [[Battle for Lion's Arch]] 2022 re-release. == Trivia == * Internally at ArenaNet, this area was known as "Valley_Arch." * The composite image in the loading screen includes parts of the [[wikipedia:Széchenyi Chain Bridge|Széchenyi Chain Bridge]] in Budapest, including one of its iconic lions. * Story instances from the [[Personal Story]] that take place on Lion's Arch use the original appearance of the city, prior to the events of [[Living World Season 1]]. Further, a sepia filter is added which can be disabled by turning post-processing off. This sepia filter also affects the cinematic that welcomes players to their chosen [[Order]]; players might wish to disable post-processing to watch the original cinematics without the filter. == See also == * [[Memory of Old Lion's Arch]] * [[The Battle For Lion's Arch]] ** [[Lion's Arch (Under Attack!)]] (Original version from [[Escape from Lion's Arch]]) ** [[Lion's Arch (Enemy Controlled)]] (Original version from [[Battle for Lion's Arch/archive|Battle for Lion's Arch]]) * [[Sea of Sorrows (book)]] == External links == {{gww}} * [http://web.archive.org/web/20110514121229/http://www.arena.net/blog/lions-arch-building-the-heart-of-the-city Lion's Arch: Building the Heart of the City – Archived copy of the article from ArenaNet's Blog] * [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=v0MzZyZ-ty8 The City of Lion's Arch - YouTube] * [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=u7VrDhEnzFQ Guild Wars 2 - Escape from Lion's Arch Destruction Cinematic - YouTube] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q75uHc42mo4 Guild Wars 2 - Welcome to the New Lion's Arch - YouTube] * [https://www.guildwars2.com/en/news/hidden-arcana-designing-the-new-lions-arch Hidden Arcana: Designing the New Lion’s Arch] {{Cities nav}} [[Category:Ghosts of Ascalon locations]] [[Category:Edge of Destiny locations]] [[Category:Sea of Sorrows locations]] [[de:Löwenstein]] [[es:Arco del León]] [[fr:L'Arche du lion]]

{{otheruses|the modern area|the original version|Hooligan's Route (Memory of Old Lion's Arch)|historical information about the area|Hooligan's Route/historical}} {{Location infobox | points-of-interest = 1 | vistas = 1 | type = Area | sector id = 859 | within = Lion's Arch | gallery1 = The Undermarket.jpg | gallery1-text = The Undermarket | connections = [[Commodore's Quarter]] (E)
[[White Crane Terrace]] (S)
[[Western Ward]] (SE) }} [[Hooligan's Route]] is a cave located in northwest [[Lion's Arch]], north of [[Western Ward]]. [[The Undermarket]] can be found within. == Locations == ;Points of Interest : {{map objective|point of interest|The Undermarket|id=1064}} ;Vistas : {{map objective|vista|The Undermarket Vista|id=1396}} ::From [[Western Ward]] Waypoint head towards the Undermarket. There are a series of stepped stalagmites sticking up from the ground. Jump to the highest stalagmite in series. Jump to the rocky platforms until you reach the vista. Note you don't actually need to jump onto the narrow vista stalagmite. If you creep forward as far as you can on the last large stalagmite then you can activate the vista from there. == NPCs == === Allies === ;[[Ambient creature]]s * [[Rat]] ;[[Human]]s * [[Ravious]] ;[[Norn]] * [[Sonder the Seller]] ;[[Skritt]] * [[Pirate King Drrikk'ttikk]] * [[Skritt Bottle Lobber]] * [[Skritt Gear Lobber]] * [[Skritt Forager]] * [[Skritt Pistoleer]] * [[Skritt Sentry]] === Services === : {{vendor|festival}} [[April Fools' Vendor]] : {{vendor|birthday vendor}} [[Birthday Vendor]] : {{vendor|misc|Dragon Bash.png}} [[Dragon Bash Vendor]] : {{vendor|weaponsmith}} [[Frakktukk]] : {{vendor|festival}} [[Four Winds Vendor]] : {{vendor|festival}} [[Fortune Scrap Vendor]] : {{vendor|merchant}} [[Gharr Leadclaw]] : {{vendor|halloween}} [[Halloween Vendor]] : {{vendor|misc|Lunar New Year Vendor.png}} [[Lunar New Year Vendor]] : {{vendor|wintersday}} [[Wintersday Vendor]] : {{vendor|merchant}} [[Namrrokk]] : {{vendor|misc|HoMPS merchant (map icon).png}} [[Sarettokk]] : {{vendor|merchant}} [[Shiny King M'takk]] : {{vendor|festival}} [[Sovereign Weapon Vendor]] : {{vendor|SAB}} [[Super Adventure Box Trader]] : {{vendor|SAB}} [[Super Adventure Box Weaponsmith]] : {{vendor|armorsmith}} [[Uttorrchakk]] : {{vendor|lw merchant}} [[Zotturrikk]] == Ambient dialogue == ''For previous dialogue, see [[Hooligan's Route/dialogue]].'' ;Ravious tending to his rats :'''Ravious:''' ''Here, take it!'' :'''Ravious:''' ''There you go!'' == Objects == * [[Tri-key Chest]] == Notes == * The karma vendor [[Sarettokk]] sells/gives players [[Hall of Monuments Portal Stone]]s which enables them to visit an otherwise unreachable site in the game, the [[Hall of Monuments]]. * After residents were evacuated during [[Escape from Lion's Arch]], [[skritt]] moved in and inhabited the area around The Undermarket. When the former residents came back and attempted to reclaim the area, the skritt chased them away, of which included: ** A [[Blood Legion Soldier]], [[Citizen]], [[Covington Pirate]], [[Crab]], [[Pirate (NPC)|Pirate]], as well as [[Bort the Bloodless]], [[Naima Ice-veined]], [[Black Davi]], [[Gullveig the Grim]], [[Scornheart]], and [[Salty Emil]]. * There was a karma vendor [[Gwenwyn]] who sold Pirate-styled level 40 {{rarity|rare}} weapons found here. She was removed from the game and replaced with [[First Mate Shane]] who can be found at the exit of the [[Weyandt's Revenge]] jumping puzzle. == Trivia == * Was originally named "New Town Center" in [[:File:Valley Arch sectormap.jpg|early maps]] of Lion's Arch. {{Lion's Arch nav}} [[Category:Skritt culture]] [[de:Weg des Rohlings]] [[es:Ruta del Vándalo]] [[fr:Route du gredin]]
